title: Code Structuring
tag: Module 09 · The Model / Function / Controller Split
scan: What you will learn: The Model/Function/Controller split — how to assign every piece of code to exactly one of three responsibilities
Why it matters: Code without structural assignment is impossible to review at scale — the split makes the scope of any change predictable before reading the code
Model files have no side effects / Function files have no state / Controller files have no business logic — if any file violates one rule, refactor before adding features
This structure is the prerequisite for Stalwart — Stalwart formalises and extends these three roles into eight file types

blocks.html: ow fold it together. Take the mental model from Module 00 and let it dictate where each kind of code lives. You arrive — without forcing it — at a clean three-way split:

cards.title: Model
cards.body: The shape of your information. Collections, their heads, their relationships.

cards.title: Function
cards.body: Logic decides, render shows, helpers assist — the three types of Module 03.

cards.title: Controller
cards.body: Events route through here. It listens, then tells the right function to run.

blocks.html: Model holds it. Functions do it. The Controller decides when. Three questions, three homes — structure is just discipline about where answers go.
That is the structural core of Part I. One module left — the one that bridges everything you've just learned into the architect's view in Part III.

sections.title: Enforce the split at the folder

blocks.html: DEPT® field pattern — use folder structure to enforce the split: model/, fn/, ctrl/ directories at the component root. A misplaced file is visible in a PR diff without reading the code. Add a CI grep that alerts on import of a model file from inside a fn/ file and vice versa.

blocks.label: Architect's Review · Code Structuring
items: Is the model / function / controller split visible at file level?
Where has it leaked — and what is the migration plan?
Do new features arrive in this shape by default?
What lint, test or PR rule enforces the split?
